attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- statusline: Set up Claude Code's status line UI
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
- insights: Generate a report analyzing your Claude Code sessions
- team-onboarding: Help teammates ramp on Claude Code with a guide from your usage

name: Prefer real JS scaffolds over static HTML

type: feedback
---

When asked to build a web page or site, scaffold a real JavaScript project with `npx` (e.g., `npx create-next-app`, `npm create vite@latest`) rather than writing standalone HTML/CSS files.

**Why:** User explicitly corrected this mid-task — "can you build a real javascript site? use npx to init the project and go from there." They want something that resembles a real project, not a throwaway HTML page, even for quick demos.

**How to apply:** Default to a modern JS scaffold for any "build me a web page/site" request unless the user specifies otherwise (e.g., "plain HTML"). Pick the scaffold based on fit — Next.js if routing/multiple pages are needed, Vite+React for SPAs, etc. Don't ask; just pick a sensible default and go.

content.text:
Build passes cleanly. Here's what you've got in `/Users/jason/playground/devdash-demo`:

**Scaffold:** Next.js 16 (App Router) + TypeScript + Tailwind, created via `create-next-app`.

**Two navigable pages:**
- `/` — hero intro with tagline and two CTAs (`src/app/page.tsx`)
- `/features` — four feature cards with a "back to home" link (`src/app/features/page.tsx`)
- Shared header nav + footer live in `src/app/layout.tsx`

**Two planted UI errors** (visible, non-breaking — good demo targets):
1. **Typo in the hero H1** — `src/app/page.tsx:13` says "collaboraton" (missing the `i`). It's front and center on the home page.
2. **Odd-one-out feature card** — `src/app/features/page.tsx:35` gives the third card a yellow border/background while the other three are neutral. Obvious visual inconsistency.

A bonus latent issue you may or may not want to catch: the footer in `layout.tsx` reads "© 2023 DevDash" (today is 2026). Happy to leave, fix, or swap for something else.

Run it with `npm run dev` when you're ready. Let me know when you want to `devdash init` the project.

content.content:
DevDash CLI — lightweight task tracking built for AI coding agents and developer workflows.

Usage:
  devdash [command]

Available Commands:
  activity        View activity log
  admin           Admin commands (requires ADMIN_SECRET)
  agent-setup     Configure agent instructions for this repository
  alias-setup     Add 'dd' alias to your shell RC file
  analyze         Trigger sandbox analysis for an issue
  blocked         Pending issues with unsatisfied dependencies
  close           Close one or more issues
  comment         Add a comment to an issue
  comments        List comments on an issue
  completion      Generate the autocompletion script for the specified shell
  create          Create a new issue
  delete          Delete one or more issues
  dep             Manage dependencies between issues
  diagnose        Investigate bead: status, job history, failure details
  dispatch        Dispatch a bead for execution
  doctor          Check configuration and connectivity
  find            Look up a bead by full UUID across all projects
  help            Help about devdash or a specific topic
  import          Import GitHub issues
  init            Initialize devdash in the current repository
  jobs            List recent jobs
  list            List issues
  login           Authenticate with DevDash
  prime           Output AI-optimized workflow context for agent injection
  project         Manage projects
  ready           Pending, unblocked issues sorted by priority
  reconcile-tasks Audit and fix backlog inconsistencies
  report          Report progress on an issue
  score           Score beads for automability
  self-update     Update devdash to the latest version
  show            Full issue detail
  stale           In-progress issues with no recent activity
  stats           Project health: open/closed/blocked counts
  sync            Trigger full GitHub reconciliation
  team            List project team members
  token           Manage API tokens
  uninstall       Remove devdash and its configuration
  update          Update an issue
  version         Print the version

Flags:
  -h, --help             help for devdash
      --project string   Override project ID (full UUID or prefix)

Use "devdash [command] --help" for more information about a command.

content.content:
Create a new issue in the current project.

Requires --title. Optionally set the type (task, bug, feature,
enhancement, thought), priority (0=critical through 4=backlog),
description, parent issue, due date, and time estimate.

The new issue is created in "pending" status. Use "devdash update"
to mark it in_progress when you begin work.

Usage:
  devdash create [flags]

Flags:
      --description string   Issue description
      --due string           Due date (YYYY-MM-DD)
      --estimate int         Estimated minutes
  -h, --help                 help for create
      --parent string        Parent bead ID
      --priority int         Priority: 0=critical, 1=high, 2=medium, 3=low, 4=backlog (default 2)
      --title string         Issue title (required)
      --type string          Issue type: task, bug, feature, enhancement, thought (default "task")

Global Flags:
      --project string   Override project ID (full UUID or prefix)

Visible front-and-center above the fold at /."

See the conditional in src/app/features/page.tsx around the feature map (i === 2 branch). Remove the special-case so all four cards share the neutral card styling."

content: Building a simple DevDash intro site (Next.js, two pages) as a demo for the DevDash fix workflow, with three UI bugs planted and filed as dd's. Dev server is running at localhost:3000; next up is picking a dd and demoing a fix. (disable recaps in /config)
